overall.description: First-person cockpit view from an AS350 helicopter flying through dense fog and storm clouds in a flight simulator. The visibility gradually deteriorates from light gray fog to near-black conditions as the aircraft progresses through the weather system, with only the instrument panel and nose antenna visible against the featureless clouds.

scene_state.functional_zone: helicopter cockpit in white fog conditions

agents.ego_description: piloting the helicopter steadily through dense white fog, maintaining course using flight instruments

entered.detail: AS350 instrument panel with flight gauges, altimeter, and GPS navigation display
thin communication antenna protruding from the helicopter nose
dense white fog completely obscuring all external visibility beyond the aircraft nose

segments.scene_description: The segment shows a stable first-person view from the helicopter cockpit flying through uniform white fog. The instrument panel is clearly visible in the foreground with the antenna mast visible ahead, while the outside world is completely obscured by dense white clouds.

scene_state.functional_zone: helicopter cockpit entering darker storm clouds

events.description: ambient light decreases significantly as helicopter enters darker gray cloud layer

agents.ego_description: continuing flight forward as visibility conditions deteriorate and fog darkens to gray

segments.scene_description: The helicopter continues flying through thick clouds as the ambient lighting dims significantly. The white fog transitions to dark gray storm clouds, reducing visibility further while the instrument panel remains illuminated and the antenna mast stays visible against the darkening background.

scene_state.functional_zone: helicopter cockpit in heavy storm darkness

events.description: external visibility drops to near zero as conditions become almost completely dark

agents.ego_description: flying through extremely dark storm conditions with minimal external visibility, relying entirely on instruments

segments.scene_description: The final segment shows the helicopter entering extremely dark conditions where the storm clouds become nearly black. External visibility is reduced to almost zero, with only the illuminated instrument panel and the faint outline of the antenna mast visible against the darkness.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person perspective from a helicopter cockpit as the surrounding environment descends from a bright white haze into an oppressive, near-black storm. It begins with the aircraft gliding through uniform white fog, where the instrument panel and nose antenna are clearly defined against a featureless, bright backdrop. As the flight progresses, the ambient lighting dims significantly and the white fog transitions into heavy, dark gray storm clouds. The scene then shifts toward extreme darkness as the clouds deepen into a nearly black void, reducing external visibility to almost zero. Finally, the view settles into a state of total obscurity, leaving only the illuminated glow of the instrument panel and the faint outline of the antenna mast visible against the dark.
